text.Rotherham: For, even when we came into Macedonia, no relief at all, had our flesh; but, in every way, were we in tribulation,—without, fightings! within, fears!
text.KJV: For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were troubled on every side; without were fightings, within were fears.
text.NABRE: For even when we came into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were afflicted in every way—external conflicts, internal fears.
text.DRB: For also when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we suffered all tribulation: without, combats; within, fears.

text.NRSV: But God, who consoles the downcast, consoled us by the arrival of Titus,
text.Rotherham: But, he who encourageth them that are brought low, encouraged us,—even God,—by the presence of Titus.
text.KJV: Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;
text.NABRE: But God, who encourages the downcast, encouraged us by the arrival of Titus,
text.DRB: But God that comforteth the humble, did comfort us, in the coming of Titus.

text.NRSV: and not only by his coming, but also by the consolation with which he was consoled about you, as he told us of your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
text.Rotherham: Not, however, by his presence alone, but also by the encouragement wherewith he had been encouraged over you: recounting unto us your earnest desire, your lamentation, your zeal in my behalf. So that I the more rejoiced.
text.KJV: And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.
text.NABRE: and not only by his arrival but also by the encouragement with which he was encouraged in regard to you, as he told us of your yearning, your lament, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced even more.
text.DRB: And not only in his coming, but also in the consolation, wherewith he was comforted among you, reporting to us your desire, your weeping, your emulation for me, so that I rejoiced the more.

text.NRSV: For even if I made you sorry with my letter, I do not regret it (though I did regret it, for I see that I grieved you with that letter, though only briefly).
text.Rotherham: Because, if I even grieved you by the letter, I do not regret,—though I could even have regretted,—I see that that letter, if even for an hour, did cause you grief.
text.KJV: For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.
text.NABRE: For even if I saddened you by my letter, I do not regret it; and if I did regret it ([for] I see that that letter saddened you, if only for a while),
text.DRB: For although I made you sorry in an epistle, it repenteth me not: albeit it repented me, seeing that the same epistle (although but for a time) did make you sorry.

text.NRSV: Now I rejoice, not because you were grieved, but because your grief led to repentance; for you felt a godly grief, so that you were not harmed in any way by us.
text.Rotherham: Now, am I rejoicing,—not that ye were grieved, but that ye were grieved unto repentance; for ye were grieved according to God, in order that, in nothing, should ye receive damage from us.
text.KJV: Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made sorry after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.
text.NABRE: I rejoice now, not because you were saddened, but because you were saddened into repentance; for you were saddened in a godly way, so that you did not suffer loss in anything because of us.
text.DRB: Now I am glad: not because you were made sorry, but because you were made sorry to penance. For you were made sorry according to God, that in nothing you should suffer detriment by us.

text.NRSV: For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation and brings no regret, but worldly grief produces death.
text.Rotherham: For, grief according to God, worketh, repentance unto salvation, not to be regretted; although, the grieving of the world, worketh, death.
text.KJV: For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not to be repented of: but the sorrow of the world worketh death.
text.NABRE: For godly sorrow produces a salutary repentance without regret, but worldly sorrow produces death.
text.DRB: For the sorrow that is according to God, worketh penance unto salvation that is stable: but the sorrow of the world worketh death.

text.NRSV: For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you, what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation, what alarm, what longing, what zeal, what punishment! At every point you have proved yourselves guiltless in the matter.
text.Rotherham: For lo! this very thing—the being caused to grieve, according to God:—what manner of diligence it wrought out in you,—nay! defence,—nay! sore displeasure,—nay! fear,—nay! earnest desire,—nay! jealousy,—nay! avenging. In every way, ye shewed yourselves to be, chaste, in the matter.
text.KJV: For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, yea, what vehement desire, yea, what zeal, yea, what revenge! In all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter.
text.NABRE: For behold what earnestness this godly sorrow has produced for you, as well as readiness for a defense, and indignation, and fear, and yearning, and zeal, and punishment. In every way you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
text.DRB: For behold this very thing, that you were made sorry according to God, how great carefulness it worketh in you: yea defence, yea indignation, yea fear, yea desire, yea emulation, yea revenge, in all things you have shewed yourselves to be undefiled in the matter.

text.NRSV: So although I wrote to you, it was not on account of the one who did the wrong, nor on account of the one who was wronged, but in order that your zeal for us might be made known to you before God.
text.Rotherham: Hence, if I even wrote unto you, it was not for the sake of him that did the wrong, [nay] not even for the sake of him that suffered the wrong; but for the sake of your earnestness, which was on our account, being made manifest unto you, before God:-
text.KJV: Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might appear unto you.
text.NABRE: So then even though I wrote to you, it was not on account of the one who did the wrong, or on account of the one who suffered the wrong, but in order that your concern for us might be made plain to you in the sight of God.
text.DRB: Therefore although I wrote to you, not for him that did the injury, nor for him that suffered: but to manifest our carefulness that we have for you before God,

text.NRSV: In this we find comfort. In addition to our own consolation, we rejoiced still more at the joy of Titus, because his mind has been set at rest by all of you.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, have we received encouragement. In addition to our encouragement, however, much more abundantly, have we rejoiced over the joy of Titus,—that his spirit hath received refreshment from you all,
text.KJV: Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was refreshed by you all.
text.NABRE: For this reason we are encouraged. And besides our encouragement, we rejoice even more because of the joy of Titus, since his spirit has been refreshed by all of you.
text.DRB: therefore we are comforted. But in our consolation, we did the more aboundantly rejoice upon the joy of Titus, because his spirit was refreshed of all you.

text.NRSV: For if I have been somewhat boastful about you to him, I was not disgraced; but just as everything we said to you was true, so our boasting to Titus has proved true as well.
text.Rotherham: That, if in anything—unto him—in your behalf—I have boasted, I have not been put to shame; but, as, all things, in truth, we told you, so, even our boasting before Titus, turned out to be, truth.
text.KJV: For if I have boasted any thing to him of you, I am not ashamed; but as we spake all things to you in truth, even so our boasting, which I made before Titus, is found a truth.
text.NABRE: For if I have boasted to him about you, I was not put to shame. No, just as everything we said to you was true, so our boasting before Titus proved to be the truth.
text.DRB: And if to him I gloried any thing of you, I am not confounded: but as we spake all things to you in truth, so also our glorying that was to Titus, is made a truth.

text.NRSV: And his heart goes out all the more to you, as he remembers the obedience of all of you, and how you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
text.Rotherham: And, his tender affections, are, much more abundantly towards you, when he calleth to mind the obedience, of you all,—how, with fear and trembling, ye gave him welcome.
text.KJV: And his inward affection is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.
text.NABRE: And his heart goes out to you all the more, as he remembers the obedience of all of you, when you received him with fear and trembling.
text.DRB: And his bowels are more aboundantly toward you: remembering the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling you received him.

text.NRSV: I rejoice, because I have complete confidence in you.
text.Rotherham: I rejoice that, in everything, I am of good courage respecting you.
text.KJV: I rejoice therefore that I have confidence in you in all things.
text.NABRE: I rejoice, because I have confidence in you in every respect.
text.DRB: I rejoice that in all things I have confidence in you.

text.NRSV: We want you to know, brothers and sisters, about the grace of God that has been granted to the churches of Macedonia;
text.Rotherham: Moreover we make known unto you, brethren, the favour of God which hath been given in the assemblies of Macedonia,—
text.KJV: Moreover, brethren, we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;
text.NABRE: We want you to know, brothers, of the grace of God that has been given to the churches of Macedonia,
text.DRB: And we do you to understand, brethren, the grace of God, that is given in the Churches of Macedonia,

text.NRSV: for during a severe ordeal of affliction, their abundant joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
text.Rotherham: That, in a great testing of tribulation, the superabounding of their joy and their deep destitution, superabounded unto the riches of their liberality;
text.KJV: How that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality.
text.NABRE: for in a severe test of affliction, the abundance of their joy and their profound poverty overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
text.DRB: that in much experience of tribulation they had aboundance of joy, & their very deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their simplicity,

text.NRSV: For, as I can testify, they voluntarily gave according to their means, and even beyond their means,
text.Rotherham: That, according to power, I bear witness, and beyond power, of their own accord, [they acted],—
text.KJV: For to their power, I bear record, yea, and beyond their power they were willing of themselves;
text.NABRE: For according to their means, I can testify, and beyond their means, spontaneously,
text.DRB: for according to their power (I give them testimony) and above their power they were willing,

text.NRSV: begging us earnestly for the privilege of sharing in this ministry to the saints—
text.Rotherham: With much exhortation, entreating of us the favour and the fellowship of the ministry which was for the saints;—
text.KJV: Praying us with much intreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.
text.NABRE: they begged us insistently for the favor of taking part in the service to the holy ones,
text.DRB: with much exhortation requesting us the grace and communication of the ministry that is done toward the saints.

text.NRSV: and this, not merely as we expected; they gave themselves first to the Lord and, by the will of God, to us,
text.Rotherham: And, not merely as we hoped, but, themselves, gave they, first, unto the Lord and unto us through God’s will,
text.KJV: And this they did, not as we hoped, but first gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.
text.NABRE: and this, not as we expected, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and to us through the will of God,
text.DRB: And not as we hoped, but their own selves they gave, first to our Lord, then to us by the will of God:

text.NRSV: so that we might urge Titus that, as he had already made a beginning, so he should also complete this generous undertaking among you.
text.Rotherham: To the end we should exhort Titus, in order that, according as he before made a beginning, so, he should also complete unto you this favour also.
text.KJV: Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.
text.NABRE: so that we urged Titus that, as he had already begun, he should also complete for you this gracious act also.
text.DRB: in so much that we desired Titus, that as he began, so also he would perfit in you this grace also.

text.NRSV: Now as you excel in everything—in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in utmost eagerness, and in our love for you —so we want you to excel also in this generous undertaking.
text.Rotherham: But, just as, in everything, ye superabound,—in faith, and discourse, and knowledge, and all earnestness, and in the love among you which proceedeth from us, in order that, in this favour also, ye would superabound.
text.KJV: Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all diligence, and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.
text.NABRE: Now as you excel in every respect, in faith, discourse, knowledge, all earnestness, and in the love we have for you, may you excel in this gracious act also.
text.DRB: But as in all things you abound in faith, and word, & knowledge, & all carefulness, moreover also in your charity toward us, that in this grace also you may abound.

text.NRSV: I do not say this as a command, but I am testing the genuineness of your love against the earnestness of others.
text.Rotherham: Not by way of injunction, do I speak, but through, others’, earnestness, and, the genuineness of your own love, putting to the test.
text.KJV: I speak not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the sincerity of your love.
text.NABRE: I say this not by way of command, but to test the genuineness of your love by your concern for others.
text.DRB: Ispeak not as commanding: but by the carefulness of others, approving also the good disposition of your charity.

text.NRSV: For you know the generous act of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, so that by his poverty you might become rich.
text.Rotherham: For ye are taking knowledge of the favour of our Lord Jesus [Christ],—how that, for your sakes, he became destitute—although he was, rich, in order that, ye, by his destitution, might be enriched.
text.KJV: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.
text.NABRE: For you know the gracious act of our Lord Jesus Christ, that for your sake he became poor although he was rich, so that by his poverty you might become rich.
text.DRB: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that for you he was made poor, whereas he was rich; that by his poverty you might be rich.

text.NRSV: And in this matter I am giving my advice: it is appropriate for you who began last year not only to do something but even to desire to do something—
text.Rotherham: And, a judgment, herein, do I give,—for, this, unto you, is profitable,—who, indeed, not only of the doing, but of the desiring, made for yourselves a beginning a year ago;—
text.KJV: And herein I give my advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.
text.NABRE: And I am giving counsel in this matter, for it is appropriate for you who began not only to act but to act willingly last year:
text.DRB: And in this point I give counsel: for this is profitable for you, which have begun not only to do, but also to be willing, from the year past:

text.NRSV: now finish doing it, so that your eagerness may be matched by completing it according to your means.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, now, the doing also, complete ye, in order that, even according to the forwardness of the desiring, so, may be the completing—out of what ye have.
text.KJV: Now therefore perform the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to will, so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have.
text.NABRE: complete it now, so that your eager willingness may be matched by your completion of it out of what you have.
text.DRB: But now perform ye it also in deed: that as your mind is prompt to be willing, so it may be also to perform, of that which you have.

text.NRSV: For if the eagerness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what one has—not according to what one does not have.
text.Rotherham: For, if the forwardness is set forth, according to what one may have, he is well approved, not according to what one hath not.
text.KJV: For if there be first a willing mind, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.
text.NABRE: For if the eagerness is there, it is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what one does not have;
text.DRB: For if the will be prompt, it is accepted according to that which it hath, not according to that which it hath not.

text.NRSV: I do not mean that there should be relief for others and pressure on you, but it is a question of a fair balance between
text.Rotherham: For, not that unto others should be relief, and unto you distress [do I speak], but, by equality, in the present season, your surplus for their deficiency,—
text.KJV: For I mean not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:
text.NABRE: not that others should have relief while you are burdened, but that as a matter of equality
text.DRB: For not that other should have ease, and you tribulation: but by an equality.

text.NRSV: your present abundance and their need, so that their abundance may be for your need, in order that there may be a fair balance.
text.Rotherham: In order that their surplus may come to be for your deficiency: that there may come about an equality:—
text.KJV: But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want: that there may be equality:
text.NABRE: your surplus at the present time should supply their needs, so that their surplus may also supply your needs, that there may be equality.
text.DRB: Let in this present time your aboundance supply their want: that their aboundance also may supply your want, that there be an equality

text.NRSV: As it is written, “The one who had much did not have too much, and the one who had little did not have too little.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—He that [gathered] the much, had not more than enough, and, he that [gathered] the little, had not less,
text.KJV: As it is written, He that had gathered much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “Whoever had much did not have more, and whoever had little did not have less.” Titus and His Collaborators.
text.DRB: as it is written: He that had much, abounded not: and he that had little, wanted not.

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God who put in the heart of Titus the same eagerness for you that I myself have.
text.Rotherham: Thanks, however, unto God!—who is putting the same earnestness in your behalf in the heart of Titus,
text.KJV: But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.
text.NABRE: But thanks be to God who put the same concern for you into the heart of Titus,
text.DRB: And thanks be to God, that hath given the self-same carefulness for you in the heart of Titus,

text.NRSV: For he not only accepted our appeal, but since he is more eager than ever, he is going to you of his own accord.
text.Rotherham: In that, though, indeed, the exhortation, he welcomed; yet already being, greatly in earnest, of his own accord, hath he gone forth unto you.
text.KJV: For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.
text.NABRE: for he not only welcomed our appeal but, since he is very concerned, he has gone to you of his own accord.
text.DRB: for that he admitted indeed exhortation: but being more careful, of his own will he went unto you.

text.NRSV: With him we are sending the brother who is famous among all the churches for his proclaiming the good news;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, we also set forward, with him, the brother, whose praise in the Glad Tidings, [hath gone] through all the assemblies:—
text.KJV: And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;
text.NABRE: With him we have sent the brother who is praised in all the churches for his preaching of the gospel.
text.DRB: We have sent also with him the brother, whose praise is in the Gospel through all the churches:

text.NRSV: and not only that, but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us while we are administering this generous undertaking for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our goodwill.
text.Rotherham: Not only so, however, but he hath also been appointed by the assemblies, as a fellow-traveler with us in this favour, which is being ministered by us with a view to the Lord’s glory and our earnest desire:—
text.KJV: And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:
text.NABRE: And not only that, but he has also been appointed our traveling companion by the churches in this gracious work administered by us for the glory of the Lord [himself] and for the expression of our eagerness.
text.DRB: & not only that, but also he was ordained of the churches fellow of our peregrination, for this grace which is ministered of us to the glory of our Lord, and our determined will:

text.NRSV: We intend that no one should blame us about this generous gift that we are administering,
text.Rotherham: Arranging this—lest anyone, upon us, should cast blame, in this munificence which is being ministered by us;
text.KJV: Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:
text.NABRE: This we desire to avoid, that anyone blame us about this lavish gift administered by us,
text.DRB: avoiding this, lest any man might reprehend us in this fullness that is ministered of us.

text.NRSV: for we intend to do what is right not only in the Lord’s sight but also in the sight of others.
text.Rotherham: For we provide things honourable, not only before [the] Lord, but also before men.
text.KJV: Providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
text.NABRE: for we are concerned for what is honorable not only in the sight of the Lord but also in the sight of others.
text.DRB: For we provide good things not only before God, but also before men.

text.NRSV: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found eager in many matters, but who is now more eager than ever because of his great confidence in you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover we have set forward, with them, our brother whom we have proved, in many things, ofttimes, earnest,—but, now, much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence [which he hath] towards you.
text.KJV: And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great confidence which I have in you.
text.NABRE: And with them we have sent our brother whom we often tested in many ways and found earnest, but who is now much more earnest because of his great confidence in you.
text.DRB: And we have sent with them our brother also, whom we have proved in many things often to be careful: but now much more careful, for the great confidence in you,

text.NRSV: As for Titus, he is my partner and co-worker in your service; as for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Whether as regardeth Titus, he is a partner of mine, and, towards you, a fellow-worker. or our brethren, apostles of assemblies, and Christ’s glory.
text.KJV: Whether any do enquire of Titus, he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be enquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, and the glory of Christ.
text.NABRE: As for Titus, he is my partner and co-worker for you; as for our brothers, they are apostles of the churches, the glory of Christ.
text.DRB: either for Titus which is my fellow and coadjutor toward you, or our brethren Apostles of the Churches, the glory of Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore openly before the churches, show them the proof of your love and of our reason for boasting about you.
text.Rotherham: The proof of your love, therefore, and of our boasting in your behalf, shew ye, unto them, in the face of the assemblies.
text.KJV: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.
text.NABRE: So give proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting about you to them.
text.DRB: The declaration therefore which is of your charity and our glorying for you, declare ye toward them in the face of the churches.

text.NRSV: Now it is not necessary for me to write you about the ministry to the saints,
text.Rotherham: For, indeed, concerning the ministry which is for the saints, it is, superfluous for me, to be writing to you;
text.KJV: For as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:
text.NABRE: Now about the service to the holy ones, it is superfluous for me to write to you,
text.DRB: For concerning the ministry that is done toward the saints, it is superfluous for me to write unto you.

text.NRSV: for I know your eagerness, which is the subject of my boasting about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year; and your zeal has stirred up most of them.
text.Rotherham: For I know your forwardness of mind—of which, in your behalf, I am boasting unto Macedonians, that, Achaia, hath been prepared for a year past,—and, your zeal, hath stirred up the greater number of them.
text.KJV: For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath provoked very many.
text.NABRE: for I know your eagerness, about which I boast of you to the Macedonians, that Achaia has been ready since last year; and your zeal has stirred up most of them.
text.DRB: For I know your prompt mind: for the which I glory of you to the Macedonians: That Achaia also is ready from the year past, and your emulation hath provoked very many.

text.NRSV: But I am sending the brothers in order that our boasting about you may not prove to have been empty in this case, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless I have sent the brethren, that, our boast in your behalf, be not made void, in this respect, that, even as I was saying, ye may be prepared,—
text.KJV: Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:
text.NABRE: Nonetheless, I sent the brothers so that our boast about you might not prove empty in this case, so that you might be ready, as I said,
text.DRB: But I have sent the brethren, that the thing which we glory of you, be not made void in this behalf, that (as I have said) you may be ready:

text.NRSV: otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated—to say nothing of you—in this undertaking.
text.Rotherham: Lest by any means, if there should come with me Macedonians, and find you unprepared, we,—not to say, ye,—should be put to shame in this confidence.
text.KJV: Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me, and find you unprepared, we (that we say not, ye) should be ashamed in this same confident boasting.
text.NABRE: for fear that if any Macedonians come with me and find you not ready we might be put to shame (to say nothing of you) in this conviction.
text.DRB: lest when the Macedonians shall come with me, and find you unready, we (that we say not, ye) may be ashamed in this substance.

text.NRSV: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you, and arrange in advance for this bountiful gift that you have promised, so that it may be ready as a voluntary gift and not as an extortion.
text.Rotherham: Necessary, therefore, I regarded it, to exhort the brethren that they would go forward unto you, and make up beforehand your previously- promised blessing. The same, to be ready—thus, as a blessing, and not as of constraint.
text.KJV: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your bounty, whereof ye had notice before, that the same might be ready, as a matter of bounty, and not as of covetousness.
text.NABRE: So I thought it necessary to encourage the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for your promised gift, so that in this way it might be ready as a bountiful gift and not as an exaction.
text.DRB: Therefore I thought it necessary to desire the brethren that they would come to you, and prepare this blessing before promised, to be ready so, as a blessing, not as avarice.

text.NRSV: The point is this: the one who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
text.Rotherham: As to this, however, he that soweth sparingly, sparingly, also shall reap, and, he that soweth with blessings, with blessings, also shall reap:
text.KJV: But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully.
text.NABRE: Consider this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
text.DRB: And this I say, he that soweth sparingly, sparingly also shall reap: and he that soweth in blessings, of blessings also shall reap.

text.NRSV: Each of you must give as you have made up your mind, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
text.Rotherham: Each one, according as he hath purposed in his heart, not sorrowfully, nor of necessity,—for, a cheerful giver, God, loveth.
text.KJV: Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.
text.NABRE: Each must do as already determined, without sadness or compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
text.DRB: Every one as he hath determined in his heart, not of sadness or of necessity. For God loveth a cheerful giver.

text.NRSV: And God is able to provide you with every blessing in abundance, so that by always having enough of everything, you may share abundantly in every good work.
text.Rotherham: Moreover God is able to cause, every gracious gift, to superabound unto you, in order that, having in every thing, at every time, every kind of sufficiency of your own, ye may be superabounding unto every good work;
text.KJV: And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:
text.NABRE: Moreover, God is able to make every grace abundant for you, so that in all things, always having all you need, you may have an abundance for every good work.
text.DRB: And God is able to make all grace abound in you: that in all things always having all sufficiency, you may abound unto all good works,

text.NRSV: As it is written, “He scatters abroad, he gives to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—He hath scattered abroad, he hath given to the needy,—his righteousness, abideth to futurity.
text.KJV: (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “He scatters abroad, he gives to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”
text.DRB: as it is written: He distributed, he gave to the poor: his justice remaineth for ever.

text.NRSV: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Now, he that supplieth seed to the sower, and bread for eating, will supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and cause to grow your fruits of righteousness:
text.KJV: Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;)
text.NABRE: The one who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
text.DRB: And he that ministereth seed to the sower, will give bread also for to eat: and will multiply your seed and will augment the increases of the fruits of your justice:

text.NRSV: You will be enriched in every way for your great generosity, which will produce thanksgiving to God through us;
text.Rotherham: In every thing, being enriched, unto every kind of liberality,—which, indeed, worketh out, through us, thanksgiving unto God;
text.KJV: Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us thanksgiving to God.
text.NABRE: You are being enriched in every way for all generosity, which through us produces thanksgiving to God,
text.DRB: that being enriched in all things, you may abound unto all simplicity, which worketh by us thanks-giving to God.

text.NRSV: for the rendering of this ministry not only supplies the needs of the saints but also overflows with many thanksgivings to God.
text.Rotherham: Because, the ministry of this public service, is not only towards filling up the deficiencies of the saints, but also superabounding, through the thanksgiving of many, unto God,—
text.KJV: For the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;
text.NABRE: for the administration of this public service is not only supplying the needs of the holy ones but is also overflowing in many acts of thanksgiving to God.
text.DRB: Because the ministry of this office doth not only supply those things that the Saints want, but aboundeth also by many thanks-givings in our Lord,

text.NRSV: Through the testing of this ministry you glorify God by your obedience to the confession of the gospel of Christ and by the generosity of your sharing with them and with all others,
text.Rotherham: [Of many] who, through the proof of this ministry, are glorifying God for your acknowledged submission unto the glad-message of the Christ, and for the liberality of the fellowship, unto them, and unto all,—
text.KJV: Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal distribution unto them, and unto all men;
text.NABRE: Through the evidence of this service, you are glorifying God for your obedient confession of the gospel of Christ and the generosity of your contribution to them and to all others,
text.DRB: by the proof of this ministry, glorifying God in the obedience of your confession unto the Gospel of Christ, and in the simplicity of communicating unto them, and unto all,

text.NRSV: while they long for you and pray for you because of the surpassing grace of God that he has given you.
text.Rotherham: Themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, longing after you, by reason of the surpassing favour of God [resting] upon you:—
text.KJV: And by their prayer for you, which long after you for the exceeding grace of God in you.
text.NABRE: while in prayer on your behalf they long for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.
text.DRB: and in their praying for you, being desirous of you because of the excellent grace of God in you.

text.NRSV: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!
text.Rotherham: Thanks be unto God, for his unspeakable bounty!
text.KJV: Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
text.NABRE: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!
text.DRB: Thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift.

text.NRSV: I myself, Paul, appeal to you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ—I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away!—
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I, Paul, myself, exhort you, through the meekness and considerateness of the Christ,—I who, to look upon, indeed, am lowly toward you, but, absent, am bold towards you;—
text.KJV: Now I Paul myself beseech you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ, who in presence am base among you, but being absent am bold toward you:
text.NABRE: Now I myself, Paul, urge you through the gentleness and clemency of Christ, I who am humble when face to face with you, but brave toward you when absent,
text.DRB: And I Paul myself beseech you by the mildness and modesty of Christ, who in presence indeed am humble among you, but absent am bold on you.

text.NRSV: I ask that when I am present I need not show boldness by daring to oppose those who think we are acting according to human standards.
text.Rotherham: I entreat, however, that, when present, I may not be bold with the assurance wherewith I think to be daring against some who account of us as though, according to flesh, we were walking,—
text.KJV: But I beseech you, that I may not be bold when I am present with that confidence, wherewith I think to be bold against some, which think of us as if we walked according to the flesh.
text.NABRE: I beg you that, when present, I may not have to be brave with that confidence with which I intend to act boldly against some who consider us as acting according to the flesh.
text.DRB: But I beseech you, that being present I need not be bold by that confidence wherewith I am thought to be bold against some: which think us as though we walk according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: Indeed, we live as human beings, but we do not wage war according to human standards;
text.Rotherham: For, though, in flesh, we walk, not, according to flesh, do we war,—
text.KJV: For though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh:
text.NABRE: For, although we are in the flesh, we do not battle according to the flesh,
text.DRB: For walking in the flesh, we war not according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: for the weapons of our warfare are not merely human, but they have divine power to destroy strongholds. We destroy arguments
text.Rotherham: For, the weapons of our warfare, are not fleshly, but mighty, by God, unto a pulling down of strongholds,—
text.KJV: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)
text.NABRE: for the weapons of our battle are not of flesh but are enormously powerful, capable of destroying fortresses. We destroy arguments
text.DRB: For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal: but mighty to God unto the destruction of munitions, destroying counsels,

text.NRSV: and every proud obstacle raised up against the knowledge of God, and we take every thought captive to obey Christ.
text.Rotherham: When we pull down, calculations, and every height that uplifteth itself against the knowledge of God, and when we bring into captivity every thought unto the obedience of the Christ,
text.KJV: Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ;
text.NABRE: and every pretension raising itself against the knowledge of God, and take every thought captive in obedience to Christ,
text.DRB: and all loftiness extolling itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity all understanding unto the obedience of Christ,

text.NRSV: We are ready to punish every disobedience when your obedience is complete.
text.Rotherham: And when we hold ourselves, in readiness, to avenge all disobedience, as soon as your obedience shall be fulfilled!
text.KJV: And having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.
text.NABRE: and we are ready to punish every disobedience, once your obedience is complete.
text.DRB: and having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience, when your obedience shall be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: Look at what is before your eyes. If you are confident that you belong to Christ, remind yourself of this, that just as you belong to Christ, so also do we.
text.Rotherham: The things that lie on the surface, ye are looking at:—if anyone hath come to trust in himself that he is, Christ’s, this, let him reckon, again, with himself—that, even as, he, is Christ’s, so, also are, we.
text.KJV: Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ’s, let him of himself think this again, that, as he is Christ’s, even so are we Christ’s.
text.NABRE: Look at what confronts you. Whoever is confident of belonging to Christ should consider that as he belongs to Christ, so do we.
text.DRB: See the things that are according to appearance. If any man have affiance in himself, that he is Christ's: let him think this again with himself, that as he is Christ's, so we also.

text.NRSV: Now, even if I boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for tearing you down, I will not be ashamed of it.
text.Rotherham: Yea, if, somewhat more abundantly, I should boast concerning our authority—which the Lord hath given for building up and not for pulling you down, I shall not be put to shame,—
text.KJV: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority, which the Lord hath given us for edification, and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:
text.NABRE: And even if I should boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for tearing you down, I shall not be put to shame.
text.DRB: For and if I should glory some-what more of our power, which our Lord hath given us unto edification and not to your destruction; I shall not be ashamed.

text.NRSV: I do not want to seem as though I am trying to frighten you with my letters.
text.Rotherham: That I may not seem as though I would be terrifying you through means of my letters;
text.KJV: That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.
text.NABRE: May I not seem as one frightening you through letters.
text.DRB: But that I may not be thought as it were to terrify you by epistles

text.NRSV: For they say, “His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.”
text.Rotherham: Because, The letters, it is true (saith one), are weighty and strong, but, the presence of the body, is weak, and, the discourse, contemptible;—
text.KJV: For his letters, say they, are weighty and powerful; but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.
text.NABRE: For someone will say, “His letters are severe and forceful, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.”
text.DRB: (for his epistles indeed, say they, are sore and vehement; but his bodily presence weak, & his speech contemptible)

text.NRSV: Let such people understand that what we say by letter when absent, we will also do when present.
text.Rotherham: This, let such a one reckon—that, what we are, in our word, through means of letters, being absent, such, also, being present, are we, in our deed.
text.KJV: Let such an one think this, that, such as we are in word by letters when we are absent, such will we be also in deed when we are present.
text.NABRE: Such a person must understand that what we are in word through letters when absent, that we also are in action when present.
text.DRB: let him this think that is such a one, that such as we are in word by epistles, absent: such also we are in deed, present.

text.NRSV: We do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another, and compare themselves with one another, they do not show good sense.
text.Rotherham: For we dare not class or compare ourselves with some who do, themselves, commend; but, they, among themselves, measuring, themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without discernment!
text.KJV: For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among themselves, are not wise.
text.NABRE: Not that we dare to class or compare ourselves with some of those who recommend themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another, they are without understanding.
text.DRB: For we dare not match or compare ourselves with certain, that commend themselves: but we measure ourselves in ourselves, & compare ourselves to ourselves.

text.NRSV: We, however, will not boast beyond limits, but will keep within the field that God has assigned to us, to reach out even as far as you.
text.Rotherham: We, however, not as to the things without measure, will boast ourselves, but, according to the measure of the limit which God apportioned unto us, as a measure—to reach as far as even you!
text.KJV: But we will not boast of things without our measure, but according to the measure of the rule which God hath distributed to us, a measure to reach even unto you.
text.NABRE: But we will not boast beyond measure but will keep to the limits God has apportioned us, namely, to reach even to you.
text.DRB: But we will not glory above our measure: but according to the measure of the rule; which God hath measured to us, a measure to reach even unto you.

text.NRSV: For we were not overstepping our limits when we reached you; we were the first to come all the way to you with the good news of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, not as though we were not reaching unto you, are we over-stretching ourselves, for, as far as even you, were we beforehand in the glad-message of the Christ:
text.KJV: For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure, as though we reached not unto you: for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of Christ:
text.NABRE: For we are not overreaching ourselves, as though we did not reach you; we indeed first came to you with the gospel of Christ.
text.DRB: For not, as though we reached not unto you, do we extend ourselves beyond. For we are come as far as to you in the Gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: We do not boast beyond limits, that is, in the labors of others; but our hope is that, as your faith increases, our sphere of action among you may be greatly enlarged,
text.Rotherham: Not, as to the things without measure, boasting ourselves in other men’s toils, but having, hope—your faith, growing—among you, to be enlarged, according to our limit for something beyond,—
text.KJV: Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other men’s labours; but having hope, when your faith is increased, that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule abundantly,
text.NABRE: We are not boasting beyond measure, in other people’s labors; yet our hope is that, as your faith increases, our influence among you may be greatly enlarged, within our proper limits,
text.DRB: not glorying above measure in other men's labours: but having hope of your faith increasing, to be magnified in you according to our rule aboundantly,

text.NRSV: so that we may proclaim the good news in lands beyond you, without boasting of work already done in someone else’s sphere of action.
text.Rotherham: Unto the regions beyond you, to carry the glad-message: not, within another man’s limit, as to the things made ready, to boast ourselves.
text.KJV: To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another man’s line of things made ready to our hand.
text.NABRE: so that we may preach the gospel even beyond you, not boasting of work already done in another’s sphere.
text.DRB: yea unto those places that are beyond you, to evangelize, not in another man's rule, to glory in those things that are prepared before.

text.NRSV: “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: He that boasteth, however, in the Lord, let him boast;
text.KJV: But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.
text.NABRE: “Whoever boasts, should boast in the Lord.”
text.DRB: But he that glorieth, let him glory in our Lord.

text.NRSV: For it is not those who commend themselves that are approved, but those whom the Lord commends.
text.Rotherham: For, not he that commendeth himself, he, is approved, but he whom, the Lord, doth commend.
text.KJV: For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.
text.NABRE: For it is not the one who recommends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord recommends.
text.DRB: For not he that commendeth himself, the same is approved; but whom God commendeth.

text.NRSV: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
text.Rotherham: I could wish ye would bear with me as to some little foolishness,—Nay! do even bear with me!
text.KJV: Would to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.
text.NABRE: If only you would put up with a little foolishness from me! Please put up with me.
text.DRB: Would God you could bear some little of my folly: but do ye also support me:

text.NRSV: I feel a divine jealousy for you, for I promised you in marriage to one husband, to present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
text.Rotherham: For I am jealous over you with a jealousy, of God; for I myself betrothed you unto one husband, to present, a chaste virgin, unto the Christ,—
text.KJV: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
text.NABRE: For I am jealous of you with the jealousy of God, since I betrothed you to one husband to present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
text.DRB: for I emulate you with the emulation of God. For I have despoused you to one man, to present you a chaste virgin unto Christ.

text.NRSV: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by its cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
text.Rotherham: But I fear lest, by any means, as, the serpent, completely deceived Eve, in his craftiness, your minds should be corrupted from the singleness [and the chasteness] which are [due] unto the Christ.
text.KJV: But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
text.NABRE: But I am afraid that, as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts may be corrupted from a sincere [and pure] commitment to Christ.
text.DRB: But I fear lest, as the serpent seduced Eve by his subtlety, so your senses may be corrupted, & fall from the simplicity that is in Christ.

text.NRSV: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you submit to it readily enough.
text.Rotherham: For, if, indeed, he that cometh, is proclaiming, another Jesus, whom we have not proclaimed, or, a different Spirit, ye are receiving, which ye had not received, or a different glad-message, which ye have never welcomed, ye are, well, bearing.
text.KJV: For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or if ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another gospel, which ye have not accepted, ye might well bear with him.
text.NABRE: For if someone comes and preaches another Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it well enough.
text.DRB: For if he that cometh, preach another Christ whom we have not preached, or you receive another spirit whom you have not received; or another Gospel which you have not received: you might well suffer it.

text.NRSV: I think that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
text.Rotherham: For I reckon not to have come a whit behind the exceeding overmuch apostles;
text.KJV: For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.
text.NABRE: For I think that I am not in any way inferior to these “superapostles.”
text.DRB: For I suppose that I have done nothing less than the great Apostles.

text.NRSV: I may be untrained in speech, but not in knowledge; certainly in every way and in all things we have made this evident to you.
text.Rotherham: And, even if uncultured in my discourse, certainly not in my knowledge,—but, in every way, having made [it] manifest, in all things, unto you.
text.KJV: But though I be rude in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.
text.NABRE: Even if I am untrained in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.
text.DRB: For although rude in speech, yet not in knowledge. But in all things we are made manifest to you.

text.NRSV: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I proclaimed God’s good news to you free of charge?
text.Rotherham: Or, a sin, did I commit—abasing, myself, that, ye, might be exalted,—in that, free of charge, God’s glad-message, I announced unto you?
text.KJV: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?
text.NABRE: Did I make a mistake when I humbled myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you without charge?
text.DRB: Or did I commit a sin, humbling myself, that you might be exalted? because I evangelized unto you the Gospel of God gratis?

text.NRSV: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
text.Rotherham: Other assemblies, I despoiled, receiving supplies that I might minister, unto you;
text.KJV: I robbed other churches, taking wages of them, to do you service.
text.NABRE: I plundered other churches by accepting from them in order to minister to you.
text.DRB: Other churches I spoiled, taking a stipend, for your ministry.

text.NRSV: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for my needs were supplied by the friends who came from Macedonia. So I refrained and will continue to refrain from burdening you in any way.
text.Rotherham: And, being present with you, and having come short, I was not burdensome to anyone,—for, my deficiency, the brethren, coming from Macedonia, helped to make up;—and, in everything, without burden unto you, I kept, myself—and will keep!
text.KJV: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied: and in all things I have kept myself from being burdensome unto you, and so will I keep myself.
text.NABRE: And when I was with you and in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my needs. So I refrained and will refrain from burdening you in any way.
text.DRB: And when I was with you, and had need, I was burdenous to none: for that which I wanted, the brethren supplied that came from Macedonia: and in all things I have kept myself without burden to you, and will keep.

text.NRSV: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boast of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
text.Rotherham: Truth of Christ is in me, that, this boasting, shall not be silenced unto me, in the regions of Achaia.
text.KJV: As the truth of Christ is in me, no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
text.NABRE: By the truth of Christ in me, this boast of mine shall not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
text.DRB: The truth of Christ is in me, that this glorying shall not be infringed toward me in the countries of Achaia.

text.NRSV: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
text.Rotherham: Wherefore? Because I love you not? God, knoweth!
text.KJV: Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.
text.NABRE: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
text.DRB: Wherefore? because I love you not? God doth know.

text.NRSV: And what I do I will also continue to do, in order to deny an opportunity to those who want an opportunity to be recognized as our equals in what they boast about.
text.Rotherham: But, what I am doing, I also will do,—that I may cut off the occasion of them who are wishing an occasion, that, wherein they are boasting themselves, they may be found even as also, we.
text.KJV: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion; that wherein they glory, they may be found even as we.
text.NABRE: And what I do I will continue to do, in order to end this pretext of those who seek a pretext for being regarded as we are in the mission of which they boast.
text.DRB: But that which I do, I will also do, that I may cut away the occasion of them that desire occasion: that, in that which they glory, they may be found even like us.

text.NRSV: For such boasters are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, such as these, are false apostles, deceitful workers, transfiguring themselves into apostles of Christ.
text.KJV: For such are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into the apostles of Christ.
text.NABRE: For such people are false apostles, deceitful workers, who masquerade as apostles of Christ.
text.DRB: For such false apostles are crafty workers, transfiguring themselves into Apostles of Christ.

text.NRSV: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
text.Rotherham: And no marvel! for, Satan himself, doth transfigure himself into a messenger of light!
text.KJV: And no marvel; for Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.
text.NABRE: And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.
text.DRB: And no marvel: for Satan himself transfigureth himself into an Angel of light.

text.NRSV: So it is not strange if his ministers also disguise themselves as ministers of righteousness. Their end will match their deeds.
text.Rotherham: No great thing, therefore, if, his ministers also, are transfiguring themselves as ministers of righteousness!—whose end, shall be according to their works.
text.KJV: Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness; whose end shall be according to their works.
text.NABRE: So it is not strange that his ministers also masquerade as ministers of righteousness. Their end will correspond to their deeds.
text.DRB: It is no great matter therefore if his ministers be transfigured as the ministers of justice: whose end shall be according to their works.

text.NRSV: I repeat, let no one think that I am a fool; but if you do, then accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
text.Rotherham: Again, I say—let no one deem me to be, foolish; but, otherwise, at least, as foolish, give me welcome, that, I also, some little, may boast myself.
text.KJV: I say again, Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool receive me, that I may boast myself a little.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Boast: His Labors. I repeat, no one should consider me foolish; but if you do, accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
text.DRB: Again I say, (let no man think me to be foolish: otherwise take me as foolish, that I also may glory a little,)

text.NRSV: What I am saying in regard to this boastful confidence, I am saying not with the Lord’s authority, but as a fool;
text.Rotherham: What I am saying, not according to the Lord, am I saying, but as in foolishness,—in this my boastful confidence!
text.KJV: That which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.
text.NABRE: What I am saying I am not saying according to the Lord but as in foolishness, in this boastful state.
text.DRB: that which I speak, I speak not according to God, but as it were in foolishness, in this substance of glorying.

text.NRSV: since many boast according to human standards, I will also boast.
text.Rotherham: Since, many, are boasting after the flesh, I also, will boast;
text.KJV: Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.
text.NABRE: Since many boast according to the flesh, I too will boast.
text.DRB: Because many glory according to the flesh, I also will glory.

text.NRSV: For you gladly put up with fools, being wise yourselves!
text.Rotherham: For, gladly, do ye bear with the foolish, being [yourselves], discreet,—
text.KJV: For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye yourselves are wise.
text.NABRE: For you gladly put up with fools, since you are wise yourselves.
text.DRB: For you do gladly suffer the foolish: whereas yourselves are wise.

text.NRSV: For you put up with it when someone makes slaves of you, or preys upon you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or gives you a slap in the face.
text.Rotherham: For ye bear with it—if anyone enslaveth you, if any devoureth, if anyone taketh, if anyone lifteth himself up, if anyone, on the face, doth smite you!
text.KJV: For ye suffer, if a man bring you into bondage, if a man devour you, if a man take of you, if a man exalt himself, if a man smite you on the face.
text.NABRE: For you put up with it if someone enslaves you, or devours you, or gets the better of you, or puts on airs, or slaps you in the face.
text.DRB: For you suffer if a man bring you into servitude, if a man devour, if a man take, if a man be extolled, if a man strike you on the face.

text.NRSV: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone dares to boast of—I am speaking as a fool—I also dare to boast of that.
text.Rotherham: By way of disparagement, am I speaking,—as though, we, had been weak! whereas, in whatsoever anyone dareth, in foolishness I speak, I also, dare:—
text.KJV: I speak as concerning reproach, as though we had been weak. Howbeit whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak foolishly,) I am bold also.
text.NABRE: To my shame I say that we were too weak! But what anyone dares to boast of (I am speaking in foolishness) I also dare.
text.DRB: Ispeak according to dishonour, as though we had been weak in this part. Wherein any man dare (I speak foolishly) I dare also.

text.NRSV: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
text.Rotherham: Hebrews, are they? I also, Israelites, are they? I also, seed of Abraham, are they? I also,
text.KJV: Are they Hebrews? so am I. Are they Israelites? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.
text.NABRE: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
text.DRB: They are Hebrews: and I. They are Israelites: and I. They are the seed of Abraham; and I.

text.NRSV: Are they ministers of Christ? I am talking like a madman—I am a better one: with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless floggings, and often near death.
text.Rotherham: Ministers of Christ, are they? As one deranged I am talking, I, above measure!—in toils, superabundantly, in prisons, superabundantly, in stripes, to excess, in deaths, oft,—
text.KJV: Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as a fool) I am more; in labours more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths oft.
text.NABRE: Are they ministers of Christ? (I am talking like an insane person.) I am still more, with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, far worse beatings, and numerous brushes with death.
text.DRB: They are the ministers of Christ; and I. (I speak as one scarce wise) more I: in many more labours, in prisons more aboundantly, in stripes above measure, in deaths often.

text.NRSV: Five times I have received from the Jews the forty lashes minus one.
text.Rotherham: From Jews, five times, forty-save-one, have I received,
text.KJV: Of the Jews five times received I forty stripes save one.
text.NABRE: Five times at the hands of the Jews I received forty lashes minus one.
text.DRB: Of the Jews five times, did I receive forty, saving one.

text.NRSV: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I received a stoning. Three times I was shipwrecked; for a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
text.Rotherham: Thrice, have I been beaten with rods, once, have I been stoned, thrice, have I been shipwrecked, a day and night, in the deep, have I spent;
text.KJV: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once was I stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck, a night and a day I have been in the deep;
text.NABRE: Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned, three times I was shipwrecked, I passed a night and a day on the deep;
text.DRB: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once I was stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck; night and day have I been in the depth of the sea,

text.NRSV: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from bandits, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers and sisters;
text.Rotherham: In journeyings oft, in perils of rivers, in perils of robbers, in perils from my own race, in perils from Gentiles, in perils in the city, in perils in the desert, in perils by the sea, in perils among false brethren,
text.KJV: In journeyings often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils by mine own countrymen, in perils by the heathen, in perils in the city, in perils in the wilderness, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren;
text.NABRE: on frequent journeys, in dangers from rivers, dangers from robbers, dangers from my own race, dangers from Gentiles, dangers in the city, dangers in the wilderness, dangers at sea, dangers among false brothers;
text.DRB: in journeying often, perils of waters, perils of thieves, perils of my Nation, perils of Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brethren,

text.NRSV: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, hungry and thirsty, often without food, cold and naked.
text.Rotherham: in toil and hardship, in watchings, oft, in hunger and thirst, in fasting, oft, in cold and nakedness:—
text.KJV: In weariness and painfulness, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.
text.NABRE: in toil and hardship, through many sleepless nights, through hunger and thirst, through frequent fastings, through cold and exposure.
text.DRB: in labour and misery, in much watchings, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness,

text.NRSV: And, besides other things, I am under daily pressure because of my anxiety for all the churches.
text.Rotherham: Apart from the things without, my daily care,—my anxiety for all the assemblies;—
text.KJV: Beside those things that are without, that which cometh upon me daily, the care of all the churches.
text.NABRE: And apart from these things, there is the daily pressure upon me of my anxiety for all the churches.
text.DRB: beside those things which are outwardly: my daily instance, the carefulness of all churches.

text.NRSV: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I am not indignant?
text.Rotherham: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and, I, am not burning?
text.KJV: Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?
text.NABRE: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is led to sin, and I am not indignant? Paul’s Boast: His Weakness.
text.DRB: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is scandalized, and I am not burnt?

text.NRSV: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
text.Rotherham: If, to boast, is needful, in the things that concern my weakness, will I boast.
text.KJV: If I must needs glory, I will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities.
text.NABRE: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
text.DRB: If I must glory: I will glory of the things that concern my infirmity.

text.NRSV: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus (blessed be he forever!) knows that I do not lie.
text.Rotherham: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus, knoweth—He who is blessed unto the ages—that I am not speaking falsely:
text.KJV: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.
text.NABRE: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus knows, he who is blessed forever, that I do not lie.
text.DRB: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who is blessed for ever, knoweth that I lie not.

text.NRSV: In Damascus, the governor under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
text.Rotherham: In Damascus, the governor under Aretas the king, was guarding the city of Damascenes, to apprehend me,—
text.KJV: In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desirous to apprehend me:
text.NABRE: At Damascus, the governor under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus, in order to seize me,
text.DRB: At Damascus the Governor of the nation under Aretas the king, kept the city of the Damascenes for to apprehend me:

text.NRSV: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and escaped from his hands.
text.Rotherham: And, through a window, was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.
text.KJV: And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.
text.NABRE: but I was lowered in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped his hands.
text.DRB: and through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and so escaped his hands.

text.NRSV: It is necessary to boast; nothing is to be gained by it, but I will go on to visions and revelations of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: To be boasting, is needful,—it is not, indeed, profitable, yet will I come to visions and revelations of [the] Lord:—
text.KJV: It is not expedient for me doubtless to glory. I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.
text.NABRE: I must boast; not that it is profitable, but I will go on to visions and revelations of the Lord.
text.DRB: IF I must glory (it is not expedient indeed) but I will come to the visions & revelations of our Lord.

text.NRSV: I know a person in Christ who fourteen years ago was caught up to the third heaven—whether in the body or out of the body I do not know; God knows.
text.Rotherham: I know a man in Christ, who, fourteen years ago, whether in the body, I know not, or out of the body, I know not, God, knoweth, such a one as this, caught away, as far as the third heaven;
text.KJV: I knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago, (whether in the body, I cannot tell; or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;) such an one caught up to the third heaven.
text.NABRE: I know someone in Christ who, fourteen years ago (whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows), was caught up to the third heaven.
text.DRB: I know a man in Christ above fourteen years ago (whether in the body, I know not, or out of the body, I know not: God doth know) such a one rapt even to the third Heaven.

text.NRSV: And I know that such a person—whether in the body or out of the body I do not know; God knows—
text.Rotherham: And I know such a man as this, whether in the body or apart from the body, [I know not],—God, knoweth,
text.KJV: And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)
text.NABRE: And I know that this person (whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows)
text.DRB: And I know such a man (whether in the body, or out of the body, I know not; God doth know)

text.NRSV: was caught up into Paradise and heard things that are not to be told, that no mortal is permitted to repeat.
text.Rotherham: How that he was caught away into paradise, and heard unspeakable things, which it is not allowable for a man to utter:
text.KJV: How that he was caught up into paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.
text.NABRE: was caught up into Paradise and heard ineffable things, which no one may utter.
text.DRB: that he was rapt into Paradise; & heard secret words, which it is not lawful for a man to speak.

text.NRSV: On behalf of such a one I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except of my weaknesses.
text.Rotherham: On behalf of such a one as this, will I boast, but, on behalf of myself, will I not boast, save in my weaknesses;—
text.KJV: Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not glory, but in mine infirmities.
text.NABRE: About this person I will boast, but about myself I will not boast, except about my weaknesses.
text.DRB: For such an one I will glory: but for myself I will glory nothing, saving in my infirmities.

text.NRSV: But if I wish to boast, I will not be a fool, for I will be speaking the truth. But I refrain from it, so that no one may think better of me than what is seen in me or heard from me,
text.Rotherham: Although in fact, if I should wish to boast, I shall not be foolish, for, the truth, would I speak; but I forbear, lest anyone, unto me, should reckon above what he beholdeth me [to be], or heareth from me,—even by the exceeding greatness of the revelations.
text.KJV: For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a fool; for I will say the truth: but now I forbear, lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me to be, or that he heareth of me.
text.NABRE: Although if I should wish to boast, I would not be foolish, for I would be telling the truth. But I refrain, so that no one may think more of me than what he sees in me or hears from me
text.DRB: For and if I will glory, I shall not be foolish: for I shall say truth. But I spare, lest any man should esteem me above that which he seeth in me, or heareth any thing of me.

text.NRSV: even considering the exceptional character of the revelations. Therefore, to keep me from being too elated, a thorn was given me in the flesh, a messenger of Satan to torment me, to keep me from being too elated.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, lest I should be unduly lifted up, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, a messenger of Satan, that he might buffet me,—lest I should be unduly lifted up.
text.KJV: And lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me, lest I should be exalted above measure.
text.NABRE: because of the abundance of the revelations. Therefore, that I might not become too elated, a thorn in the flesh was given to me, an angel of Satan, to beat me, to keep me from being too elated.
text.DRB: And lest the greatness of the revelations might extol me, there was given me a prick of my flesh, an angel of Satan, to buffet me.

text.NRSV: Three times I appealed to the Lord about this, that it would leave me,
text.Rotherham: In this behalf, thrice, besought I, the Lord, that it might depart from me;
text.KJV: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.
text.NABRE: Three times I begged the Lord about this, that it might leave me,
text.DRB: For the which thing thrice I besought our Lord, that it might depart from me.

text.NRSV: but he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness.” So, I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may dwell in me.
text.Rotherham: And at once he said unto me—Sufficient for thee, is my favour, for, my power, in weakness, is made complete. Most gladly, therefore, will I rather boast in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may spread a tent over me.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
text.NABRE: but he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness.” I will rather boast most gladly of my weaknesses, in order that the power of Christ may dwell with me.
text.DRB: And he said to me: My grace sufficeth thee, for power is perfited in infirmity. Gladly therefore will I glory in mine infirmity, that the power of Christ may dwell in me.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and calamities for the sake of Christ; for whenever I am weak, then I am strong.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I take pleasure in weaknesses, in insults, in necessities, in persecutions, and straits, in behalf of Christ; for, as soon as I am weak, then, am powerful.
text.KJV: Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities, in reproaches, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christ’s sake: for when I am weak, then am I strong.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and constraints, for the sake of Christ; for when I am weak, then I am strong. Selfless Concern for the Church.
text.DRB: For the which cause I please myself in infirmities, in contumelies, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christ. For when I am weak, then am I mighty.

text.NRSV: I have been a fool! You forced me to it. Indeed you should have been the ones commending me, for I am not at all inferior to these super-apostles, even though I am nothing.
text.Rotherham: I have become foolish,—ye, compelled me:—I, in fact, ought, by you, to have been commended; for, not a whit, have I come behind the exceeding overmuch apostles,—even if I am nothing:
text.KJV: I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very chiefest apostles, though I be nothing.
text.NABRE: I have been foolish. You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you. For I am in no way inferior to these “superapostles,” even though I am nothing.
text.DRB: I am become foolish: you have compelled me. For I ought to have been commended of you: for I have been nothing less than they that are above measure Apostles: although I am nothing.

text.NRSV: The signs of a true apostle were performed among you with utmost patience, signs and wonders and mighty works.
