text.Rotherham: The signs, indeed, of an apostle, were wrought out among you, in all endurance,— [both] in signs, and wonders, and mighty works:
text.KJV: Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signs, and wonders, and mighty deeds.
text.NABRE: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with all endurance, signs and wonders, and mighty deeds.
text.DRB: Yet the signs of my Apostleship have been done upon you in all patience, in signs & wonders & mighty deeds.

text.NRSV: How have you been worse off than the other churches, except that I myself did not burden you? Forgive me this wrong!
text.Rotherham: For what is there wherein ye were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies,—save that, I myself, would not allow myself to be a burden unto you? Forgive me this wrong!
text.KJV: For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches, except it be that I myself was not burdensome to you? forgive me this wrong.
text.NABRE: In what way were you less privileged than the rest of the churches, except that on my part I did not burden you? Forgive me this wrong!
text.DRB: For what is there that you have had less than the other churches; but that I myself have not burdened you? Pardon me this injury.

text.NRSV: Here I am, ready to come to you this third time. And I will not be a burden, because I do not want what is yours but you; for children ought not to lay up for their parents, but parents for their children.
text.Rotherham: Lo! this third time, I am holding myself, in readiness, to come unto you, and I will not allow myself to be a burden; for I seek not yours, but you;—for, the children, ought not to lay up, for the parents, but the parents for the children;—
text.KJV: Behold, the third time I am ready to come to you; and I will not be burdensome to you: for I seek not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.
text.NABRE: Now I am ready to come to you this third time. And I will not be a burden, for I want not what is yours, but you. Children ought not to save for their parents, but parents for their children.
text.DRB: Behold, now the third time I am ready to come to you; & I will not be burdenous unto you. For I seek not the things that are yours, but you. For neither ought the children lay up treasures for the parents, but the parents for the children.

text.NRSV: I will most gladly spend and be spent for you. If I love you more, am I to be loved less?
text.Rotherham: And, I, most gladly, will spend, and be fully spent, for your souls:—If I, exceedingly, love you, am I, the less, loved?
text.KJV: And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.
text.NABRE: I will most gladly spend and be utterly spent for your sakes. If I love you more, am I to be loved less?
text.DRB: But I most gladly will bestow, & will myself moreover be bestowed for your souls: although loving you more, I am loved less.

text.NRSV: Let it be assumed that I did not burden you. Nevertheless (you say) since I was crafty, I took you in by deceit.
text.Rotherham: But let it be!—I, myself, did not burden you,—Notwithstanding, being crafty, with guile, I caught you?
text.KJV: But be it so, I did not burden you: nevertheless, being crafty, I caught you with guile.
text.NABRE: But granted that I myself did not burden you, yet I was crafty and got the better of you by deceit.
text.DRB: But be it so: I have not burdened you: but being crafty, I took you by guile.

text.NRSV: Did I take advantage of you through any of those whom I sent to you?
text.Rotherham: Was there, anyone of those whom I have sent unto you, through whom I have overreached you?
text.KJV: Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?
text.NABRE: Did I take advantage of you through any of those I sent to you?
text.DRB: Have I circumvented you by any of them whom I sent to you?

text.NRSV: I urged Titus to go, and sent the brother with him. Titus did not take advantage of you, did he? Did we not conduct ourselves with the same spirit? Did we not take the same steps?
text.Rotherham: I exhorted Titus, and sent with him the brother,—Did, Titus, overreach you? Was it not, in the same spirit, we walked? Was it not, in the same steps?
text.KJV: I desired Titus, and with him I sent a brother. Did Titus make a gain of you? walked we not in the same spirit? walked we not in the same steps?
text.NABRE: I urged Titus to go and sent the brother with him. Did Titus take advantage of you? Did we not walk in the same spirit? And in the same steps? Final Warnings and Appeals.
text.DRB: I requested Titus, and I sent with him a brother. Did Titus circumvent you? walked we not with one spirit? not in the self-same steps?

text.NRSV: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves before you? We are speaking in Christ before God. Everything we do, beloved, is for the sake of building you up.
text.Rotherham: All this time, think ye, that, unto you, we are making a defence? Before God in Christ, we speak;—but all these things, beloved, for your upbuilding.
text.KJV: Again, think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you? we speak before God in Christ: but we do all things, dearly beloved, for your edifying.
text.NABRE: Have you been thinking all along that we are defending ourselves before you? In the sight of God we are speaking in Christ, and all for building you up, beloved.
text.DRB: Of old think you that we excuse ourselves to you? Before God, in Christ we speak: but all things (my dearest) for your edifying.

text.NRSV: For I fear that when I come, I may find you not as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish; I fear that there may perhaps be quarreling, jealousy, anger, selfishness, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.
text.Rotherham: For I fear—lest, by any means, when I come, not such as I wish, should I find you, and, I, should be found by you, such as ye do not wish;—lest, by any means, [I should find] strife, jealousy, outbursts of wrath, factions, railings, whisperings, puffed up pretensions, confusions;—
text.KJV: For I fear, lest, when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and that I shall be found unto you such as ye would not: lest there be debates, envyings, wraths, strifes, backbitings, whisperings, swellings, tumults:
text.NABRE: For I fear that when I come I may find you not such as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish; that there may be rivalry, jealousy, fury, selfishness, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.
text.DRB: For I fear lest perhaps when I come, I find you not such as I would: and I be found of you, such an one as you would not. Lest perhaps contentions, emulations, stomachings, dissensions, detractions, whisperings, swellings, seditions be among you.

text.NRSV: I fear that when I come again, my God may humble me before you, and that I may have to mourn over many who previously sinned and have not repented of the impurity, sexual immorality, and licentiousness that they have practiced.
text.Rotherham: Lest, when I again come, my God should humble me in regard to you,—and I should grieve over many who had before sinned, and not repented of the impurity, and fornication, and wantonness which they had committed.
text.KJV: And lest, when I come again, my God will humble me among you, and that I shall bewail many which have sinned already, and have not repented of the uncleanness and fornication and lasciviousness which they have committed.
text.NABRE: I fear that when I come again my God may humiliate me before you, and I may have to mourn over many of those who sinned earlier and have not repented of the impurity, immorality, and licentiousness they practiced.
text.DRB: Lest again when I come, God humble me among you: & I mourn many of them that sinned before, & have not done penance for the uncleanness & fornication and incontinency that they have committed.

text.KJV: This is the third time I am coming to you. In the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.
text.NABRE: “On the testimony of two or three witnesses a fact shall be established.”
text.NRSV: This is the third time I am coming to you. “Any charge must be sustained by the evidence of two or three witnesses.”
text.DRB: LO this the third time I come unto you: In the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word stand.
text.Rotherham: This third time, am I coming unto you:—at the mouth of two witnesses, or three, shall every matter be established.

text.KJV: I told you before, and foretell you, as if I were present, the second time; and being absent now I write to them which heretofore have sinned, and to all other, that, if I come again, I will not spare:
text.NABRE: I warned those who sinned earlier and all the others, and I warn them now while absent, as I did when present on my second visit, that if I come again I will not be lenient,
text.NRSV: I warned those who sinned previously and all the others, and I warn them now while absent, as I did when present on my second visit, that if I come again, I will not be lenient—
text.DRB: Iforetold and do foretell as present, and now absent, to them that sinned before, and all the rest, that if I come again, I will not spare.
text.Rotherham: I have said beforehand, and do say beforehand,—as present the second time, although now absent, unto them who before sinned, and unto all the rest,—that, if I come again, I will not spare.

text.KJV: Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me, which to you-ward is not weak, but is mighty in you.
text.NABRE: since you are looking for proof of Christ speaking in me. He is not weak toward you but powerful in you.
text.NRSV: since you desire proof that Christ is speaking in me. He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful in you.
text.DRB: Seek you an experiment of him that speaketh in me, Christ; who in you is not weak, but is mighty in you?
text.Rotherham: Since, a test, ye are seeking, of the speaking in me of Christ;—who, unto you, is not weak, but is powerful in you,—

text.KJV: For though he was crucified through weakness, yet he liveth by the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we shall live with him by the power of God toward you.
text.NABRE: For indeed he was crucified out of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. So also we are weak in him, but toward you we shall live with him by the power of God.
text.NRSV: For he was crucified in weakness, but lives by the power of God. For we are weak in him, but in dealing with you we will live with him by the power of God.
text.DRB: For although he was crucified of infirmity; yet he liveth by the power of God. For we also are weak in him: but we shall live with him by the power of God on you.
text.Rotherham: For, although he was crucified through weakness, yet is he living through God’s power,—for, although, we, are weak in him, yet shall we live with him, through God’s power [unto us] —

text.KJV: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Know ye not your own selves, how that Jesus Christ is in you, except ye be reprobates?
text.NABRE: Examine yourselves to see whether you are living in faith. Test yourselves. Do you not realize that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless, of course, you fail the test.
text.NRSV: Examine yourselves to see whether you are living in the faith. Test yourselves. Do you not realize that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless, indeed, you fail to meet the test!
text.DRB: Try your own selves if you be in the faith, prove ye yourselves. Know you not yourselves that Christ Jesus is in you, unless perhaps you be reprobates.
text.Rotherham: Be trying, yourselves, whether ye are in the faith, be putting, yourselves, to the test! Or do ye not recognise yourselves, seeing that, Jesus Christ, is in you,—unless perhaps ye fail in the testing.

text.KJV: But I trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates.
text.NABRE: I hope you will discover that we have not failed.
text.NRSV: I hope you will find out that we have not failed.
text.DRB: But I hope you know that we are not reprobates.
text.Rotherham: I hope, however, that ye shall come to know, that, we, fail not in the testing!

text.KJV: Now I pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we should appear approved, but that ye should do that which is honest, though we be as reprobates.
text.NABRE: But we pray to God that you may not do evil, not that we may appear to have passed the test but that you may do what is right, even though we may seem to have failed.
text.NRSV: But we pray to God that you may not do anything wrong—not that we may appear to have met the test, but that you may do what is right, though we may seem to have failed.
text.DRB: And we pray God, that you do no evil, not that we may appear approved, but that you may do that which is good, and we be as reprobates.
text.Rotherham: But we pray unto God, that ye may do nothing base,—not that, we, may appear, accepted, but that, ye, the honourable thing, may be doing, even though, we, should be, as it were, rejected;

text.KJV: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.
text.NABRE: For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.
text.NRSV: For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.
text.DRB: For we cannot any thing against the truth; but for the truth.
text.Rotherham: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth;

text.KJV: For we are glad, when we are weak, and ye are strong: and this also we wish, even your perfection.
text.NABRE: For we rejoice when we are weak but you are strong. What we pray for is your improvement.
text.NRSV: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. This is what we pray for, that you may become perfect.
text.DRB: For we rejoice, for that we are weak, and you are mighty. This also we pray for, your consummation.
text.Rotherham: For we rejoice—whensoever, we, are weak, and, ye, are, strong. This, also we pray for—your restoration:—

text.KJV: Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should use sharpness, according to the power which the Lord hath given me to edification, and not to destruction.
text.NABRE: I am writing this while I am away, so that when I come I may not have to be severe in virtue of the authority that the Lord has given me to build up and not to tear down.
text.NRSV: So I write these things while I am away from you, so that when I come, I may not have to be severe in using the authority that the Lord has given me for building up and not for tearing down.
text.DRB: Therefore these things I write absent: that being present I may not deal hardly according to the power which our Lord hath given me unto edification and not unto destruction.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, these things, while absent, am I writing, that I may not, when present, deal sharply—according to the authority which, the Lord, hath given me, for building up and not for pulling down.

text.KJV: Finally, brethren, farewell. Be perfect, be of good comfort, be of one mind, live in peace; and the God of love and peace shall be with you.
text.NABRE: Mend your ways, encourage one another, agree with one another, live in peace, and the God of love and peace will be with you.
text.NRSV: Finally, brothers and sisters, farewell. Put things in order, listen to my appeal, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
text.DRB: For the rest, brethren, rejoice, be perfect, take exhortation, be of one mind, have peace; and the God of peace & love shall be with you.
text.Rotherham: Finally, brethren, farewell! Be getting restored, be taking encouragement, be of one mind, live in peace,—and, the God of love and peace, will be with you.

text.KJV: Greet one another with an holy kiss.
text.NABRE: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the holy ones greet you.
text.NRSV: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the saints greet you.
text.DRB: Salute one another in a holy kiss. All the saints salute you.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye one another with a holy kiss.

text.KJV: All the saints salute you.
text.NABRE: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ and the love of God and the fellowship of the holy Spirit be with all of you.
text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Spirit be with all of you.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, and the charity of God, and the communication of the Holy Ghost be with you all. Amen.
text.Rotherham: All the saints, salute you.

text.KJV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen. The second epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi, a city of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus [Christ], and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit, be with you all.

13.14: The grace of the Lord Iesus Christ, and the loue of God, and the communion of the holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen. The second Epistle to the Corinthians, was written from Philippos a citie of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.
